fault_hypothesis: The expiry check on line 65 uses a strict inequality `>` instead of `>=`, causing entries to be considered valid at their exact expiry timestamp when they should be expired.

constraints.intent: An entry should not be considered valid at its exact expiry time.
If an entry is considered valid, the current time must be strictly before its expiry.
A value should only be returned if its expiry time is strictly after the current time.

spec.expr: (__cbfl_result is None) or (key in entries and now < entries[key][1])

constraints.intent: A non-None result implies the key exists and its entry has not yet expired.
